opendb_id.description: Unique identifier for the document (UUID v4)

speed.description: The speed of the RAM in MHz (e.g., 6000, 3200). Looks for hints of this in the product name.

form_factor.description: The physical form factor of the RAM module (e.g., 288-pin DIMM, 260-pin SO-DIMM)

quantity.description: Number of modules in the kit. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

capacity_gb.description: Capacity per module in GB. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

capacity.description: Total capacity of the RAM kit in GB. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

color.description: The color(s) of this product. Each color of the product should be included in the array. Includes PCB and heat spreader colors. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

cas_latency.description: The CAS latency of the RAM. Looks for hints of this in the product name.

timings.description: The complete timing specification of the RAM (e.g., 30-36-36-76). Looks for hints of this in the product name.

voltage.description: The operating voltage of the RAM

ecc.description: Whether the RAM supports Error-Correcting Code (e.g., Non-ECC, ECC)

registered.description: Whether the RAM is registered or unbuffered (e.g., Unbuffered, Registered, Load Reduced)

heat_spreader.description: Whether the RAM modules have a heat spreader

rgb.description: Whether the RAM has RGB lighting

height.description: The height of the RAM module in mm

general_product_information.description: General product information and retailer SKUs

manufacturer.description:
RAM manufacturer

Use the memory kit manufacturer. Examples: Corsair, G.Skill, Kingston.

series.description:
Product series name

The series is the manufacturer's marketed product line / family for the kit. Manufacturer (e.g., G.SKILL, Corsair, Kingston, Crucial, Team Group) and ram_type (e.g., DDR4, DDR5) are stored separately and used as the default grouping, so do NOT repeat them in the series. Speed (3200, 6000), CAS latency (CL16, CL30), capacity (16GB, 32GB), and module count (2x16GB) are also stored separately and MUST NOT be in the series. Color goes in `manufacturer_color`. The series string MUST be identical across kits that the manufacturer markets under the same product line, regardless of speed/capacity/timing/color. Examples: 'G.Skill Aegis Black / Red DDR4-3200 CL16 32GB (2x16GB)' → series='Aegis'. 'G.Skill Trident Z5 Neo RGB DDR5-6000 CL30 32GB' → series='Trident Z5 Neo' (RGB here is the lighting flag — only include 'RGB' in the series when it is part of a distinct product line like 'Vengeance RGB' / 'Vengeance RGB Pro' / 'Fury Beast RGB'). 'Corsair Vengeance LPX DDR4-3200 CL16 16GB' → series='Vengeance LPX'. 'Corsair Dominator Platinum RGB DDR5-7200' → series='Dominator Platinum'. 'Kingston Fury Beast DDR5-5600 32GB' → series='Fury Beast'. The series is what identifies the family on the box, regardless of speed bin or capacity.

variant.description:
Specific variant in the series

Most RAM kits should have an empty variant — speed, CAS latency, timings, capacity, modules, and color are all stored in their own fields and are NOT variants. Only fill `variant` when the manufacturer ships a genuine sub-model within the same (manufacturer, series, ram_type) group that needs its own selector (e.g., a separately marketed revision, a regional / OEM-only spin, or a distinct PCB / IC binning tier sold under the same series name). Examples: 'G.Skill Aegis DDR4-3200 CL16 32GB (Black / Red)' → variant='' (the speed/CL/capacity/color are all on their own fields). 'G.Skill Trident Z5 Neo RGB DDR5-6000 CL30 32GB' → variant='' (RGB is captured by the `rgb` flag and 'Neo' is part of the series). 'Corsair Vengeance LPX DDR4-3200 CL16 16GB' → variant=''. If you find yourself wanting to put speed, CAS, timings, capacity, kit count, or color into variant — stop and use the dedicated field instead. The variant must be unique within a (manufacturer, series, ram_type, manufacturer_color) group; if every kit in a group ends up with the same value, leave it empty.

manufacturer_color.description:
Manufacturer's official color name for this product variant.

Use the manufacturer's marketed colorway / heat-spreader finish name verbatim. RAM colorways are usually simple: 'Black', 'White', 'Silver', 'Black / Red', 'Black / White', 'Cool Grey', 'Matte Black'. For multi-tone heat spreaders, separate the tones with ' / ' (e.g., 'Black / Red', 'Black / Silver'). Branded special / collab colorways DO belong here when the manufacturer markets them as a distinct colorway and the rest of the SKU is otherwise identical: 'Royal Silver', 'Royal Gold', 'Hatsune Miku Edition', 'Halo Edition'. Most stock kits are 'Black'. Do NOT put speed, CL, capacity, RGB, or model-line names like 'Trident Z5', 'Aegis', 'Neo', 'RGB' in manufacturer_color — those belong in series / dedicated fields. Color is purely the visual heat-spreader / PCB finish.

last_manually_spec_verified_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the part specs were last manually verified by a human
